name: Ax (Adaptive Experimentation Platform)
pricing_notes: Open source (MIT); Meta/Facebook project; no API costs

performance.notes: Bayesian optimization overhead ~1-5s per iteration; scales with number of parameters and batch size

agent_readiness.notes: Meta's Adaptive Experimentation Platform for Bayesian optimization and A/B testing. Built on BoTorch (GP/Bayesian optimization). Service-oriented API (recommend trial, complete trial) or Python client. Supports multi-objective optimization, constraints, asynchronous trials, early stopping. Used at Meta for hyperparameter tuning, ML model optimization, and online experimentation. More enterprise-oriented than Optuna.

gotchas: More complex API than Optuna for simple use cases
Service API requires persistent storage for trial state
Multi-objective optimization requires understanding Pareto fronts
best_when: Asynchronous parallel hyperparameter search in distributed environments
Online A/B experimentation at scale (Meta-scale use cases)
When BoTorch-powered Gaussian processes needed over Optuna's TPE
avoid_when: Simple hyperparameter search (Optuna simpler and equally good)
Teams without PyTorch in their stack
summary: Ax is Meta's Adaptive Experimentation Platform for Bayesian optimization and A/B testing. Built on BoTorch's Gaussian process framework. Supports multi-objective optimization, constraints, asynchronous trials, and early stopping. Service-oriented API designed for distributed experimentation. More powerful than Optuna for complex multi-objective optimization but has a steeper learning curve for simple HPO.
